evidence_rea: the right to have this data transferred to another controller
evidence_reg: have the right to transmit those data to another controller without hindrance from the controller to which the personal data have been provided
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA frames portability as having the data transferred, while the REG gives the data subject the right to transmit the data and specifies this must be 'without hindrance' from the original controller; this changes how the transfer right is expressed.
REG-105 adds the condition '(b) the processing is carried out by automated means,' which is omitted in the REA, but omission alone is not a deviation.
REG-104 adds the condition '(a) the processing is based on consent ... or on a contract,' which is omitted in the REA, but omission alone is not a deviation.
